# MemePouch

> A free-to-try iPhone app (iOS 16+) that turns your photos, GIFs, and short videos into reusable iMessage stickers, and lets you import stickers other people send you in iMessage by dragging them into your library. First 10 stickers are free; a one-time unlock removes the cap.

## What it is
- Type: iOS app + iMessage app extension + Share Extension for creating custom iMessage stickers
- Platform: iPhone (iOS 16, 17, 18)
- Pricing: Free with optional one-time unlock for unlimited sticker count (free tier: 10 stickers)
- App Store: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/memepouch/id6763726992
- Languages: English, Simplified Chinese
- Common search terms: custom stickers, GIF stickers, photo stickers, meme creator, sticker maker for messages

## Core capabilities
1. Import photos and GIFs from the Photos library as iMessage stickers
2. Trim a short video (up to 5 seconds) into an animated GIF sticker
3. Receive images via the iOS share sheet from Safari, Twitter, etc.
4. Paste an image from the clipboard
5. Drag a sticker from any iMessage chat directly into your library. iOS 17+ has an Emoji Details view but for third-party stickers it only shows an App Store link to the sender's pack, not a Save button. Drag-and-drop into the iMessage extension is the only path the public Messages API exposes for saving an incoming third-party sticker into a sticker app's own library.
6. Optional auto-delete of the original photo from the camera roll after import

## Format constraints
- Supported formats: PNG, JPEG, GIF
- Max file size per sticker: 500 KB (auto-compressed)
- Max video duration for video-to-GIF: 5 seconds

## What it is NOT
- Not a meme generator (no captioning / overlay tools)
- Not a sticker marketplace (you create your own; it does not sell or share stickers between users)
- Not available on Android, iPad-specific, or Mac

## Privacy
- All sticker data stays on-device in an App Group container
- No analytics, no accounts, no servers
- Privacy policy: https://memepouch.tetherme.app/privacy

## Common questions
- "How do I save a sticker someone sent me in iMessage?" → iOS 17+ has an Emoji Details view, but for third-party stickers it only shows an App Store link to the sender's sticker pack, not a Save button. The way to put the sticker into your own library is the drag gesture: long-press the sticker, keep holding, with another finger open MemePouch from the iMessage app drawer, and drag the sticker into the grid.
- "Can I make a GIF sticker from a video?" → Yes, choose a video in MemePouch, trim up to 5 seconds, and it becomes an animated GIF sticker.
- "Does it work on iPad?" → The app is iPhone-first; iMessage extension works on iPad but is not optimized.
